card.description: Separates creator, consumer, curator, agent, and avatar memory roles across the second brain.
card.effect: Every retrieved or created object can answer who created it, who consumed it, who transformed it, and which avatar or agent can still use it.
card.risk: Thin source metadata can overstate authorship; confidence and source-system fields must remain visible.

flows.name: Black Horizon Repo-To-Ship World Loop

steps.description: The human frames the desired playable operating metaphor and names the MVP goal. The route begins with human intent. In Black Horizon, the goal is to make Hapa repositories feel like ships in an explorable fleet without pretending the visual layer has operational authority. Overwatch turns that intent into a board-backed work record with visible scope, review gates, and proof requirements.

steps.description: Overwatch records cards, starts, checkpoints, review gates, and telemetry as append-only events. The board is a projection from events, not the source of truth by itself. steps.description: The human names the shared MVP objective and asks for avatar-scoped Codex work. The human sets the priority and the definition of useful progress. The goal is to give each avatar a lane, a task card, and a verification contract.
